title: White Witch
content:
{{Top|fa|leg|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|The White Witch}} {{RepulsorliftVehicle |image=[[File:WhiteWitchPolyhedron.png]] |name=''White Witch'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[Mobquet Swoops and Speeders/Legends|Mobquet]]{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} |line= |model=[[C/L-82 landspeeder]] (modified) |type=[[Landspeeder/Legends|Landspeeder]] |cost=Not available for sale |modifier=[[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]] and [[Jord Dusat]]{{DroidsCite|The White Witch}} |sysmods=[[Joyhopper]] |length=3.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |speed=380 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|km/h]] |max alt=4 meters |maneuverability= |engine= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament= |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=*Pilot (1) *[[Astromech droid/Legends|Astromech droid]] (1) |skeleton= |passengers=1 |capacity=15 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |availability= |role=Racing speeder |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=[[Joben-Dusat Racing Team]]{{SW|url=news/this-is-podracing-sports-in-the-star-wars-galaxy-part-1|series=Sports in the Star Wars Galaxy|text=Part 1: This Is Podracing|nolive=1}} }} {{Quote|Nothing compares to the Witch.|Thall Joben|}} The '''''White Witch''''' was a [[Mobquet Swoops and Speeders/Legends|Mobquet]] [[C/L-82 landspeeder|C/L-82]] [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] that was heavily modified by the popular [[Joben-Dusat Racing Team|racing team]] of [[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]] and [[Jord Dusat]] during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. After Dusat was kidnapped by the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]], the ''White Witch'' made its debut as Joben and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] used it to [[Mission to Ingo|rescue]] the young mechanic. The team eventually made their way to [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]], where they planned to enter the ''White Witch'' in the [[Boonta Speeder Race]] in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]. However, the race was jeopardized when [[Tig Fromm]], seeking revenge, planted a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] in the ''White Witch''. Fortunately for Joben, the detonator was dislodged by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett's]] ''[[Silver Speeder]]'', which was pursuing the ''White Witch'' throughout the race, and Joben and R2-D2 went on to win. The victory was enough to earn both racers jobs with the [[Zebulon Dak Speeder Corporation/Legends|Zebulon Dak Speeder Corporation]]. ==Description== [[File:WhiteWitchCockpit.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Thall Joben and Kea Moll look at a monitor in the ''White Witch''{{'s}} cockpit.]] The [[Mobquet Swoops and Speeders/Legends|Mobquet]] [[C/L-82 landspeeder]] had been a popular model in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] shortly before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Although the popularity of the C/L-82 had declined by [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], it was still popular with [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] teens due to its reliability and ease of maintenance. This made it an ideal choice for the young [[Joben-Dusat Racing Team|racing team]] of [[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]] and [[Jord Dusat]]. The two overhauled most of the [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder's]] systems and installed several modifications to improve its performance. The finished ''White Witch'', painted white to match its name, was 3.5 meters in length and capable of traveling at 380 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers per hour]]. As a result of the [[joyhopper]] system installed by Dusat, the speeder could, for a short period of time, ride noticeably higher than an average speeder and had a maximum altitude of four meters. The speeder could also bank to allow it to negotiate tight corners. The speeder's cockpit had space for two, allowing a single passenger to ride in the vehicle, behind the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. Both driver and passenger seats were equipped with seatbelts. A socket, situated by the engines to the rear of the cockpit, was designed for an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] to assist with controlling the vehicle, while the pilot maintained control with a stick in the cockpit. A dashboard hosted a variety of other controls, mounted around a monitor that could be used to display data from the astromech droid. Another monitor and some controls were situated behind the pilot for use from the rear passenger seat.''[[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]'' opening credits The speeder also had limited cargo space, allowing it to carry 15 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]]. ==History== ===Construction=== {{Quote|We're gonna blow 'em right off the track.|Jord Dusat|}} [[File:WhiteWitchUnveiled-SWD.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Jord Dusat unveils the ''White Witch''.]]In the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the speeder racers Thall Joben and Jord Dusat came into possession of a C/L-82 landspeeder. Naming it the ''White Witch'',[[Droids (picture book)|''Droids'' Spanish picture book]] the two began modifying the speeder in their [[Ingo speeder shop|speeder shop]] on [[Ingo/Legends|Ingo]], with a view to entering it into the [[Boonta Speeder Race]], for what would be their first major competition. Over the course of three years Dusat worked as mechanic, implementing Joben's designs into the speeder as they sought to create a superior racing landspeeder. By [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],"[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]" in ''[[Polyhedron 170]]'' states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of '' A New Hope'' in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], meaning that ''Star Wars: Droids'' takes place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]. the ''White Witch'' was nearing completion, but there was one major component missing—an astromech droid to assist the driver. Fortunately for them, as Joben and Dusat raced their speeders through the [[Vaj Desert]] on Ingo, they encountered the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], who had just been dumped by their previous owner, the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Zevel Hortine]], along with his cargo as he attempted to evade the authorities. R2-D2 was ideal for the role, so the two racers became the droids' new masters. ===The Fromm Gang=== {{Quote|Hey, how's the ''Witch'' handle?''"
"''Don't you ever think of anything but racing?''"
"''What? No.|Jord Dusat and Thall Joben|}} [[File:Thewhitewitch.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''White Witch'']] As Joben and Dusat attempted to return to the speeder shop, they accidentally entered the restricted zone around the [[Tig Fromm's base|secret base]] of [[Tig Fromm]], son of [[Sise Fromm]] and prominent member of the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]]. Pursued by [[Fromm Tower Droid|sentry droids]], they were able to escape with the aid of [[Kea Moll]], a member of the [[Annoo resistance]] who was [[Mission to Ingo|investigating]] reports that Fromm was developing a powerful weapons satellite called the ''[[Trigon One]]''. However, the racers had made enemies of the Fromms, who feared that they might leak details of the secret base to rival gangs. Returning to the speeder shop, the racers planned to test R2-D2 with the ''White Witch'' the following morning. However, shortly after Joben and the droids left for the night, [[muscle droid]]s sent by Tig Fromm captured Dusat. Upon returning to the shop, Joben was told by Moll what had happened to Dusat. They soon came under attack from more muscle droids who had sabotaged Joben's speeder to prevent his escape. Acting quickly, Moll and R2-D2 activated the ''White Witch'', allowing the group to escape. Taking the ''White Witch'', Joben, Moll and the droids were able to gain entry to Fromm's base. While Moll and C-3PO guided them, Joben and R2-D2 flew the ''White Witch'' through the base's network of service tubes and elevator shafts and rescued Dusat, picking up the others as they headed for the exit. Fromm tried to stop their escape, ordering his [[droid cruiser]]s outside to await them after first disabling his sentry droids to prevent them attacking the cruisers. However, C-3PO was able to use the security system to reactivate the sentry droids. As the two groups of droids opened fire on each other, the ''White Witch'' evaded pursuing [[repulsor ball]]s and escaped. [[File:White_Witch_attacked.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''White Witch'' under attack from Fromm Tower Droids]] The incident had an unexpected bonus—Joben and Dusat had been looking for a way to transport the ''White Witch'' to [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]] for the race and now found a solution when Moll agreed to transport them on her [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Sand Sloth]]'', with the ''White Witch'' secured to the hull.{{DroidsCite|Escape Into Terror}} The journey would not run smoothly, however, and a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] malfunction caused them to head to [[Annoo (Gelefil)|Annoo]] for repairs. The Fromm Gang's spies reported the group's presence on Annoo to Sise, who vowed to deal with them himself. An attempt to capture them at the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] failed when R2-D2 created a distraction, allowing them to once again escape in the ''White Witch''. Heading for the farm owned by Moll's [[Parent/Legends|mother]], [[Demma Moll|Demma]], they stored the ''White Witch'' there for the duration of their stay. ===Arrival at Boonta=== {{Quote|Nice speeder you got there. Bit banged up, but she'll go.|Proto One|{{DroidsCite|A Race to the Finish}}}} The trip to Boonta was, however, put on hold when the young racers agreed to help Kea and Demma in their fight against the Fromms. {{Unlicensedstart}} At some point, as Tig Fromm prepared for the launch of the ''Trigon One'', Dusat and R2-D2 took the ''White Witch'', accompanied by Joben and C-3PO in another landspeeder, to where the weapons satellite was hidden among the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]]. While Joben created a distraction, Dusat and R2-D2 boarded the ship and disabled it before the group made their escape.{{StoryCite|story=Neutralizing Trigon I|book=MyComyc|bformatted=''MyComyc'' 1|sformatted=Neutralizing ''Trigon I''}} After the racers subsequently stole the ''Trigon One'', Moll was kidnapped by Sise Fromm's agents and [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] at his headquarters. Fromm demanded that Joben and Dusat return the ''Trigon One'' in exchange for Moll's release. While Joben and Dusat guarded the ''Trigon One'', R2-D2 and C-3PO took the ''White Witch'' to Fromm's headquarters and rescued Moll, safely returning her to her friends.{{StoryCite|story=Kea Kidnapped|book=MyComyc|bformatted=''MyComyc'' 2}} {{Unlicensedend}} [[File:WhiteWitchRepairs-ARttF.png|thumb|left|250px|R2-D2 carries out repairs on the White Witch before the Boonta Speeder Race.]] After destroying both the ''Trigon One'' and Sise Fromm's [[Sise Fromm's stronghold|stronghold]] on Annoo,{{DroidsCite|The Trigon Unleashed}} the group resumed their journey to Boonta. As C-3PO and R2-D2 secured the ''White Witch'' for the jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], the Fromms attempted to get revenge by attacking Moll's ship with their one remaining vessel. They succeeded in damaging the engine on the ''Sand Sloth'' but a weapons malfunction on the Fromm's rundown shuttle, the ''[[Voor Viper]]'', allowed the speeder racers to escape before the gangsters could destroy them. However, upon arrival at Boonta the damage the ship had taken caught up with them, forcing a crash landing. Though the ''White Witch'' broke free of its restraints during the crash, it survived with only minimal damage. However, Dusat, who was supposed to drive in the upcoming race, injured his arm, forcing Joben to take over. The racers also required somewhere to repair the ''White Witch'' before the race. C-3PO found a solution when a droid called [[BL-17]] offered the use of his master's garage. Unknown to the racers, BL-17 was the property of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who had been hired by Sise Fromm to extract his revenge. While Moll worked on repairing the ''White Witch'', BL-17 attempted to gas her, making it look like an accident. She escaped with the help of R2-D2. BL-17 suggested moving the ''White Witch'' to a processing plant where it would be safe until the race. However, this proved to be a trap, and Fett soon attacked Joben and Moll. During the ensuing confusion, Tig Fromm and his bodyguard, [[Vlix Oncard]], planted a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] on the speeder, which was set to go off after the tenth and final lap of the race. Joben used the ''White Witch'' to lead Fett away from the others, heading straight to the race with Fett pursuing in his own ''[[Silver Speeder]]''. However, [[Proto One]], a droid the racers had met on Boonta, saw Fromm plant the device and warned Moll. ===Boonta Speeder Race=== {{Quote|That was a fine race you won…and that is a fine speeder.|Zebulon Dak after the Boonta Speeder Race|}} [[File:WhiteWitchWins-ARttF.png|thumb|right|250px|''White Witch'' crosses the finish line of the Boonta Speeder Race.]]Fromm headed straight to the Boonta Speeder Race where his family had requisitioned a private box off its unwitting owners. By the time Moll reached the race track the ''White Witch'' was already participating in the race. With no way to warn Joben it appeared that Fromm's plan was set to work. However, Fett had followed Joben into the race and attacked him throughout, unaware of the thermal detonator. Fett fired cables to attach his speeder to the ''White Witch'' in an effort to slow it down, but R2-D2 was able to cut the cables and free the speeder. Fett then fired a blaster at the ''White Witch'', inflicting damage and causing smoke to pour from the speeder, but R2-D2 was able to patch up the damage and keep it in the race. As Joben continued to pull ahead of the competition, Fett stayed with him attacking again with a magnetic beam. Fett's attack loosened the thermal detonator, which fell off and attached to Fett's pursuing speeder. Fett ejected in time, though his speeder was destroyed. Joben went on to win the race. As Joben celebrated with his friends after the race, they were approached by [[Zebulon Dak/Legends|Zebulon Dak]], owner of [[Zebulon Dak Speeder Corporation/Legends|Zebulon Dak Speeder Corporation]]. Dak was so impressed with the ''White Witch'' that he offered Joben and Dusat full-time jobs designing and engineering speeders, an offer the two racers accepted without hesitation. ==Owners and operators== ===Thall Joben=== {{Quote|Thall spent three years working this baby, right down to the last bolt.|Jord Dusat|}} {{Main|Thall Joben/Legends}} [[File:BoontaCelebration-ARttF.png|thumb|left|250px|The Joben-Dusat Racing Team celebrate their victory.]] A native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Beheboth]], Thall Joben wanted to become a speeder racer from an early age, but was forbidden by [[Thall Joben's father|his father]], who feared that his son would come to harm. In defiance of his father, Joben traveled to Boonta in the hopes of entering the Boonta Speeder Race. Joben met Jord Dusat on Boonta and the two became friends and business partners, running a speeder shop on Ingo. Joben designed the ''White Witch'' with the Boonta Speeder Race in mind and worked together with Joben to construct the landspeeder. When Dusat was injured upon their arrival on Boonta, Joben took over piloting duties and secured victory. ===Jord Dusat=== {{Quote|Thall's the brains behind her. I just do the heavy work.|Jord Dusat|}} {{Main|Jord Dusat}} Jord Dusat was an orphan living on Boonta when he first met Thall Joben. Dusat challenged Joben to a race and was impressed when Joben defeated him by more than five [[Standard second|seconds]]. After establishing their speeder shop on Ingo, Dusat worked to construct the ''White Witch'' and was supposed to pilot the landspeeder in the Boonta Speeder Race in 15 BBY. Although an injury left Dusat unable to enter the race, Joben's victory secured both men jobs designing speeders with the Zebulon Dak Speeder Corporation. ===R2-D2=== {{Main| R2-D2/Legends}} {{Quote|Well, blast my burners, an R2 unit. Just what we've been looking for!|Jord Dusat|}} R2-D2 was an [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]] [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]].''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' R2-D2 was discovered by Joben and Dusat after he and his [[Counterpart/Legends|counterpart]], C-3PO, were abandoned by their owner, the smuggler Zevel Hortine, in the Vaj Desert on Ingo. With the two speeder racers in search of an astromech unit to operate the ''White Witch'', they recruited R2-D2 to their cause. The astromech proved his worth by assisting Joben to operate the ''White Witch'' during the rescue of Dusat from Tig Fromm's base and later the victory on Boonta. ==Behind the scenes== The ''White Witch'' was created for the [[Nelvana]] animated series ''[[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]'', lending its name to the episode in which it made its first appearance, "[[The White Witch]]," written by [[Peter Sauder]] and first broadcast on [[September 7]], [[1985]].{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} The ''Star Wars: Droids'' opening credits depicted a scene in which the ''White Witch'', flown by a white-helmeted pilot and with both C-3PO and R2-D2 aboard, attempts to evade capture by a pursuing droid. This scene is not taken from any episode of the series. Further information on the ''White Witch'' was later given in the [[2004]] [[Rich Handley]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] article ''[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]'', which appeared in ''[[Polyhedron 170]]''. The article was illustrated by [[Jeffery Carlisle|Jeff Carlisle]], who had shown a sketch of the ''White Witch'' to Erik Mona of [[Paizo Publishing]] at a convention the previous year.{{SWArchive|url=vault/collecting/jeff_carlisle_droids_revisited/index.html|text=''Droids'' Revisited: An Interview with Artist Jeff Carlisle}} Carlisle later included the ''White Witch'', along with Joben, Dusat and [[Mungo Baobab]], in the background of the official artwork for [[Celebration Anaheim (2015)|Celebration Anaheim]] in [[2015]].{{WebCite|url=http://www.jeffcarlisle.com/node/487|text=Star Wars Celebration Anaheim 2015 "Mission: Mos Eisley"|author=[[Jeffery Carlisle]]|work=JeffCarlisle.com|archivedate=20150811004436}} [[File:WhiteWitchKenner.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Early prototype of the unreleased ''White Witch'' toy]] A toy of the ''White Witch'' was intended to be released as part of [[Kenner Products|Kenner's]] [[Star Wars: Droids (toy line)|''Droids'']] action figure range. The company produced prototypes of the vehicle but, due to declining interest in ''[[Star Wars]]'' at the time, the line was canceled before the finished toy entered production.{{SWArchive|url=vault/collecting/droids_ewoks_25th_anniversary/index.html|text=25 Years of Collecting ''Droids'' and ''Ewoks''!}} In [[1986]], [[Wikipedia:Spain|Spanish]] publisher [[Editorial Gepsa]] released a [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]] called ''[[MyComyc]]'' featuring characters from popular cartoon series, including ''Star Wars: Droids'' and [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']], in a number of two-page short adventures. The comics were unknown in the [[Wikipedia:United States|United States]] until [[2013]], when [[Dark Horse Comics]] Vice President of Publishing [[Randy Stradley]] explored the possibility of republishing them. However, [[Lucasfilm]] staff were unable to find proof that the comics were officially licensed and they are considered ambiguously [[canon]].{{SW|url=news/droids-and-ewoks-return-spains-lost-star-wars-comic-strips|text=Droids and Ewoks Return: Spain's Lost ''Star Wars'' Comic Strips|int=Droids and Ewoks Return: Spain's Lost Star Wars Comic Strips|nolive=1}} The ''White Witch'' was featured in the ''MyComyc'' stories ''[[Neutralizing Trigon I]]'' and ''[[Kea Kidnapped]]''. The comics depict events which are not shown in the television series and their chronological placement is unclear. Abel G. Peña, who translated the comics to English, has commented that both stories may be alternate retellings of events in the ''Droids'' cartoon.{{JCFCite|url=posts/50635555|board=Literature|thread=Obscure Droids and Ewoks comics -- translated by Abel Peña|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2013-04-11|time=1:15 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150921222207/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/obscure-droids-and-ewoks-comics-translated-by-abel-pe%C3%B1a.50010980/page-2#post-50635555}} ==Appearances==
